prompt_text: Best FP&A that works with NetSuite and Salesforce
response:
If you need an **FP&A platform that works with both NetSuite and Salesforce**, the strongest broadly cited options are **Abacum, Datarails, Planful, Vena, Anaplan, and NetSuite Planning & Budgeting**; one recent NetSuite-focused guide also highlights **Pigment, Drivetrain, Cube, and Aleph** as current candidates depending on fit.[1][3][4][6]

For the most practical shortlist:

- **Abacum** — explicitly positioned for NetSuite integrations and also references Salesforce as part of a broader workflow automation strategy.[4]
- **Datarails** — cited as connecting to ERP systems like NetSuite and other cloud applications for automated data consolidation.[1][2]
- **Planful** — commonly included in NetSuite FP&A shortlists and is described as one of the top vendor options in NetSuite-focused evaluations.[1][2]
- **Vena** — also appears in NetSuite-integrated FP&A evaluations and is part of the standard shortlist for businesses using NetSuite.[1][6]
- **Anaplan** — included among the main NetSuite-compatible FP&A solutions in evaluation guides.[1][6]
- **NetSuite Planning & Budgeting** — the native option if you want the tightest ERP alignment with NetSuite itself.[1][2]

If your priority is **one tool that handles both NetSuite and Salesforce well**, **Abacum** is the clearest match from the search results because it is explicitly mentioned alongside both systems.[4] If your priority is **deep FP&A maturity and broader enterprise planning**, **Anaplan** and **Planful** are the more established names in the NetSuite-oriented shortlists.[1][6]

A useful way to choose is:

- Pick **Abacum** or **Datarails** if you want faster implementation and strong data consolidation around ERP/CRM workflows.[1][4]
- Pick **Planful**, **Vena**, or **Anaplan** if you want a more classic enterprise FP&A stack with broader planning sophistication.[1][6]
- Pick **NetSuite Planning & Budgeting** if staying inside the NetSuite ecosystem matters most.[1][2]

If you want, I can turn this into a **ranked recommendation by company size** or a **NetSuite + Salesforce comparison table**.
